# OpenDiabetic — The Digital Foot Lab

> Helping people keep their feet.

OpenDiabetic is a digital foot lab focused on preventing diabetic foot complications.
It helps people with diabetes catch foot trouble while it is still small — through daily
monitoring, AI-assisted review, a clinician kept in the loop, personalized offloading,
made-to-fit inserts manufactured on demand, and continuous foot-health tracking over time.

## What it is
- A specialized foot-care platform, not a general diabetes app. One thing, done well.
- The loop: daily foot photo -> AI-assisted review -> clinician confirms -> personalized
  offloading -> made-to-fit insert -> continuous tracking -> back to the daily photo.
- Private by default; a person's data stays home; a real clinician confirms uncertain cases.

## What it is not
- It does not diagnose, treat, cure, or replace a doctor. It is educational monitoring that
  supports the person and their care team — not a medical device.

## Contact
- Email: build@opendiabetic.com
- HQ: Jupiter, Florida
- Phone: 561.532.7120
- X: https://x.com/opendiabetics
- GitHub: https://github.com/SudoSuOps
- Part of the Swarm & Bee family: https://swarmandbee.com

## Key facts (every figure sourced; adversarially fact-checked)
- Up to half of people with diabetes develop nerve damage in the feet; it can dull feeling so a sore forms with no pain to warn you. (NIDDK/NIH; Diabetes Care 2023)
- 19-34% of people with diabetes develop a foot ulcer in their lifetime. (Diabetes Care 2023)
- Most diabetes-related amputations begin as a foot ulcer that, caught early, is often treatable. (Diabetes Care 2023)
- About half of diabetic foot ulcers are already infected when first seen; foot infections are the most common diabetes complication requiring hospitalization. (IDSA/IWGDF, Clin Infect Dis 2023)
- Once infection reaches the bone, amputation odds rise ~4x; with gangrene, ~11x. (PLoS One 2020; odds ratios)
- Checking foot temperature at home cut new ulcers roughly in half across five clinical trials. (pooled meta-analysis 2022 / IWGDF 2023)
- Multidisciplinary foot-care teams reduced major amputations in 94% of studies reviewed. (J Vasc Surg 2020)
- Both Type 1 (autoimmune) and Type 2 are at high risk; after a foot ulcer, Type 1 is not spared. (Diabetes Care 2022)
- About one-third of the ~$237B spent on U.S. diabetes care is tied to foot complications. (Armstrong et al. 2020, est.; ADA 2017)

## Provenance
Every clinical figure was adversarially fact-checked (multiple independent verifiers; a claim is dropped unless it survives). Refuted and unverified claims are deliberately excluded. OpenDiabetic never diagnoses; it supports the person and their care team.
